_comment: Reproduces the Verified Monitoring (N1) dashboard demo on synthetic opp 10008. The dashboard render (commcare_connect/workflow/templates/verified_monitoring_render.js) reads this curated state from the workflow run's instance.state and never fetches. 'show-don't-tell': the render presents these results neutrally with no causal claims. regenerate.py builds the full state payload (including the two-ward map overlay GeoJSON, sampled deterministically inside the REAL ward polygons) and creates a workflow run via the connect_labs MCP.
_geography_comment: Kaura (program) and Manchok (comparison) are REAL adjacent wards in Kaura LGA, Kaduna State, Nigeria. Their polygons come from the labs admin-boundary backend (/microplans/boundaries/viewport/), captured to geo/kaura_manchok.geojson. Service-delivery + survey points are sampled INSIDE these real polygons.
_coverage_comment: Six bi-monthly survey rounds per ward. Each round's verified coverage jitters within a normal survey band; the latest round (R6) equals the hero numbers. coverage_pct paired with (n surveyed, confirmed) per round.
_self_report_rounds_comment: Per-round program self-reported coverage for Kaura, plotted as a third (dashed) line on the trend. Jitters ~±10% round-to-round and sits clearly above the independently-verified line every round — the persistent overstatement. R6 = 88.0 to match the hero premium (88.0 self vs 68.1 verified = 19.9pp).
_overlay_comment: service_delivery_sample points are drawn only inside Kaura (a legible sample of the 2,300 logged visits; the count label stays 2,300). survey_pins are drawn in BOTH wards (independent survey covered both) — purple = vitamin-A confirmed, slate = not confirmed.
